_comment: Dungeons & Dragons franchise data file. All editions, games, TV/film, novels, audio, and digital media from 1974 to present.
description: The original roleplaying game, created by Gary Gygax and Dave Arneson in 1974. Defined fantasy gaming and expanded into novels, video games (60+ titles), board games, film, TV animation, and digital tools. Spans eight editions from OD&D to 5E, with settings including Forgotten Realms, Dragonlance, Greyhawk, Eberron, and Ravenloft.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons (White Box)
media.description: Original D&D boxed set. Three booklets: Men & Magic, Monsters & Treasure, The Underworld & Wilderness Adventures.
media.notes: OD&D. Created by Gary Gygax and Dave Arneson. Chainmail combat system. Out of print; available as PDF on DriveThruRPG.

media.title: dnd (PLATO)
media.description: First digital D&D adaptation. Text-based dungeon crawl on PLATO mainframe system. Inspired all later CRPGs.
media.notes: Defunct. Preserved via PLATO emulation (Cyber1). Non-canon but historically essential.

media.title: Advanced Dungeons & Dragons (1st Edition)
media.description: AD&D 1E core rulebooks: Player's Handbook, Dungeon Master's Guide, Monster Manual. Standardized the game.
media.notes: AD&D 1E. Gary Gygax primary author. Out of print; PDFs available.

media.title: Pool of Radiance
media.description: First Gold Box game. AD&D 1E rules. Set in Phlan, Forgotten Realms. Party-based tactical RPG.
media.notes: Gold Box engine. PC version playable via DOSBox. NES version censored.

media.title: Curse of the Azure Bonds
media.description: Second Gold Box game. Sequel to Pool of Radiance. Forgotten Realms setting.
media.notes: Gold Box engine. Continues from Pool of Radiance save.

media.title: Advanced Dungeons & Dragons (2nd Edition)
media.description: AD&D 2E. Revised rules, expanded settings. Planescape, Spelljammer, Dark Sun, Ravenloft.
media.notes: David Cook lead designer. Long-running era. Out of print; PDFs available.

media.title: Eye of the Beholder
media.description: First-person dungeon crawler. AD&D 2E. Undermountain setting. Seminal early CRPG.
media.notes: Trilogy: Eye of the Beholder I, II, III. SNES version is enhanced port.

media.title: Planescape: Torment
media.description: AD&D 2E. Planescape setting. Best narrative RPG ever made. 'What can change the nature of a man?'
media.notes: Enhanced Edition on GOG/Steam. Infinity Engine.

media.title: Baldur's Gate
media.description: AD&D 2E. Sword Coast campaign. Bhaalspawn storyline begins. Founded BioWare's golden era.
media.notes: Enhanced Edition available. Infinity Engine. Sequel: Baldur's Gate II (2000).

media.title: Baldur's Gate II: Shadows of Amn
media.description: AD&D 2E. Amn setting. Improved combat, deeper companion arcs. Often cited as best CRPG ever.
media.notes: Enhanced Edition available. Throne of Bhaal expansion completes the saga.

media.title: Neverwinter Nights
media.description: 3E rules. Aurora Engine. Massive modding community. Multiplayer campaigns. Neverwinter setting.
media.notes: Enhanced Edition on GOG/Steam. Diamond Edition includes all expansions.

media.title: D&D: Tower of Doom
media.description: Arcade beat-em-up. Mystara setting. Fighter, Cleric, Elf, Dwarf classes. Co-op.
media.notes: Chronicles of Mystara compilation on PS3/Xbox 360/PC.

media.title: D&D: Shadow Over Mystara
media.description: Arcade beat-em-up. Mystara setting. Sequel to Tower of Doom. Best D&D arcade game.
media.notes: Re-released on PS3/Xbox 360. Capcom arcade classics.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons (3rd Edition)
media.description: 3E core books. Unified d20 System. Skills and feats overhaul. Widespread adoption.
media.notes: Monte Cook, Jonathan Tweet, Skip Williams designers. Out of print.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons (3.5 Edition)
media.description: Revised 3E. Most popular pre-5E edition. Massive third-party content under OGL.
media.notes: Pathfinder derives from 3.5E. Out of print; PDFs available.

media.title: Neverwinter Nights: Hordes of the Underdark
media.description: Second NWN expansion. Epic levels (21-40). Underdark setting. Considered best NWN content.
media.notes: Included in Enhanced Edition.

media.title: D&D: Heroes
media.description: Console action-RPG. Forgotten Realms. Co-op hack-and-slash. Dark Alliance competitor.
media.notes: Xbox exclusive. Backward compatible on Xbox One/Series.

media.title: D&D: Forgotten Realms — Demon Stone
media.description: Action-adventure. Written by R.A. Salvatore. Voice cast: Patrick Stewart, Michael Clarke Duncan.
media.notes: 3.5E setting. Linear campaign. PC version available.

media.title: D&D Online: Stormreach
media.description: First 3.5E MMO. Eberron setting. Instanced dungeons. Free-to-play since 2009.
media.notes: Renamed Dungeons & Dragons Online. Still live, updated regularly.

media.title: Neverwinter Nights 2
media.description: 3.5E rules. Aurora 2.0 engine. Campaign set in Sword Coast. Stronghold management.
media.notes: Mask of the Betrayer expansion (2007) is critically acclaimed. GOG version available.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons (4th Edition)
media.description: 4E core books. Powers system, AEDU structure. Tactical combat focus. Divisive among fans.
media.notes: Rob Heinsoo, Andy Collins, James Wyatt designers. Out of print. Essentials line (2010) revised.

media.title: D&D: Daggerdale
media.description: 4E action-RPG. Set in Daggerdale, Forgotten Realms. Co-op. Mixed reception.
media.notes: Delisted from digital stores. No longer available new. Physical copies exist.

media.title: D&D: Neverwinter (MMO)
media.description: 4E-based MMO. Foundry user-generated content. Set in Neverwinter post-Spellplague.
media.notes: Free-to-play. Still live on PC and consoles. 5E modules added later.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons (5th Edition)
media.description: 5E core books. Streamlined rules, bounded accuracy, advantage/disadvantage. Most commercially successful edition.
media.notes: Mike Mearls, Jeremy Crawford designers. D&D Beyond digital tools. Ongoing release schedule.

media.title: Baldur's Gate: Siege of Dragonspear
media.description: Expansion bridging BG1 and BG2. AD&D 2E engine. New content, voice acting.
media.notes: Requires Baldur's Gate: Enhanced Edition. Controversial at launch but now accepted.

media.title: Idle Champions of the Forgotten Realms
media.description: Clicker/strategy game. Official D&D characters from Forgotten Realms, Dragonlance, etc. Free-to-play.
media.notes: Still active. Cross-platform progression. Features hundreds of champions.

media.title: D&D: Warriors of Waterdeep
media.description: Match-3 RPG. Waterdeep setting. Free-to-play. Multiple campaigns.
media.notes: Delisted from some stores. Server issues reported.

media.title: Baldur's Gate 3
media.description: 5E rules. Full 3D CRPG. Bhaalspawn continuation. Split-screen co-op. Game of the Year 2023.
media.notes: Critical and commercial peak. Mod support. No mobile version.

media.title: D&D: Dark Alliance
media.description: Action-RPG. Icewind Dale setting. Drizzt Do'Urden playable. Co-op focus.
media.notes: Mixed reviews. Post-launch DLC added. Still playable.

media.title: D&D: Arena of War
media.description: Free-to-play action RPG. Forgotten Realms setting. Gacha mechanics.
media.notes: Discontinued 2023. No longer downloadable. Defunct.

media.title: D&D: The Legend of Drizzt — Strategy Battle Game
media.description: Turn-based strategy. Drizzt saga. Card/unit collection. Free-to-play.
media.notes: Active. Regular content updates.

media.title: D&D: Heroes of Neverwinter
media.description: Facebook social RPG. 4E rules. Neverwinter setting. Ran 2011-2012.
media.notes: Defunct. Facebook platform closed.

media.title: D&D: Lords of Waterdeep (Digital)
media.description: Digital adaptation of the award-winning worker placement board game. Multiplayer.
media.notes: Still available on Steam and mobile. Asynchronous play.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons (Animated Series)
media.description: Cult classic cartoon. Six kids trapped in D&D world. Dungeon Master, Venger, Tiamat. 1983-1985.
media.notes: Never officially resolved finale. Available on DVD and streaming.

media.title: The Legend of Vox Machina
media.description: Animated series based on Critical Role Campaign 1. Amazon Prime Video. Action-comedy-drama. Well-received.
media.notes: Season 1 (2022): 12 eps. Season 2 (2023): 12 eps. Season 3 confirmed. D&D-adjacent canon.

media.title: D&D: The Animated Series (Retrospective)
media.description: Fan-made feature-length documentary on the 1983 series. Widely distributed.
media.notes: Non-canon. Community archive piece.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons
media.description: Theatrical film. Jeremy Irons, Marlon Wayans, Thora Birch. Critical failure; cult camp status.
media.notes: Not canon to any setting. 8% on Rotten Tomatoes.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons: Wrath of the Dragon God
media.description: Direct-to-video sequel. Different cast. Higher production quality than 2000 film.
media.notes: Syfy original. Ignored by later releases.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons: The Book of Vile Darkness
media.description: Direct-to-video third film. Loosely tied to 4E lore. Limited release.
media.notes: Low budget. Minor tie-ins to Neverwinter MMO.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons: Honor Among Thieves
media.description: Theatrical reboot. Chris Pine, Michelle Rodriguez, Regé-Jean Page. Critical and commercial success. $208M gross.
media.notes: Canon to Forgotten Realms. Set in 1490s DR. Paramount+ streaming.

media.title: Dragonlance Chronicles (Dragons of Autumn Twilight)
media.description: The foundational D&D novel trilogy. Margaret Weis and Tracy Hickman. Dragonlance setting.
media.notes: Chronicles: Autumn Twilight, Winter Night, Spring Dawning. More than 190 Dragonlance novels exist.

media.title: The Crystal Shard (Icewind Dale Trilogy)
media.description: First Drizzt Do'Urden novel. R.A. Salvatore. Icewind Dale setting. Introduced Wulfgar, Bruenor, Regis.
media.notes: Icewind Dale Trilogy: Crystal Shard, Streams of Silver, Halfling's Gem. 39+ Drizzt novels total.

media.title: Elfshadow (Harpers Series)
media.description: Elaine Cunningham. Harpers series. Arilyn Moonblade and Danilo Thann. Forgotten Realms.
media.notes: Harpers series ran 1990–1998. Out of print.

media.title: Strahd Von Zarovich: I, Strahd
media.description: P.N. Elrod. First Ravenloft novel. Jander Sunstar. Gothic horror tone.
media.notes: Ravenloft series: 30+ novels. Out of print.

media.title: Eberron: The Dreaming Dark
media.description: Keith Baker. First Eberron novels. Introduced the pulp-noir setting.
media.notes: Dreaming Dark, The Shattered Land, The Gates of Night.

media.title: Brimstone Angels
media.description: Erin M. Evans. Tiefling sisters. 4E/5E Forgotten Realms. Considered among best modern D&D fiction.
media.notes: 6 books: Brimstone Angels, Lesser Evils, The Adversary, Fire in the Blood, Ashes of the Tyrant, The Devil You Know.

media.title: The Legend of Drizzt (GraphicAudio)
media.description: Full-cast audio dramatizations. Music, sound effects. GraphicAudio production. Multiple Drizzt novels adapted.
media.notes: Ongoing series. Available on Audible and GraphicAudio direct.

media.title: Dragonlance Chronicles (Audible)
media.description: Audible-exclusive audiobooks. Narrated by Paul Boehmer. Unabridged.
media.notes: Complete Chronicles and Legends series available.

media.title: Critical Role: Vox Machina Origins (Audio)
media.description: Audio drama adaptation of Dark Horse comics. Critical Role cast. D&D adjacent canon.
media.notes: Critical Role Productions. Not official D&D canon but widely associated.

media.title: D&D Official Podcast
media.description: Wizards of the Coast official podcast. Lore discussions, designer interviews, announcements.
media.notes: 200+ episodes. Ongoing. Free on all podcast platforms.

media.title: D&D Adventure System Board Games
media.description: Cooperative dungeon crawl board games. Castle Ravenloft (2010), Wrath of Ashardalon (2011), Legend of Drizzt (2011), others.
media.notes: Standalone or mixable. Wizards of the Coast. No miniatures painting required.

media.title: Lords of Waterdeep
media.description: Worker placement board game. Set in Waterdeep. Intrigue cards, lords, quests. Highly acclaimed.
media.notes: Expansions: Scoundrels of Skullport. Digital version available.

media.title: D&D: Dungeon Mayhem
media.description: Card game. Fast-paced, 2-4 players. Iconic classes. Multiple expansion decks.
media.notes: Monster Madness, Battle for Baldur's Gate expansions.

media.title: D&D: Rock Paper Wizard
media.description: Party game. Wizards casting spells. Simultaneous action selection.
media.notes: Light filler game. Not canon.

media.title: D&D: Tyrants of the Underdark
media.description: Deck-building/area control hybrid. Underdark factions. Competitive.
media.notes: Out of print but available second-hand. Gale Force Nine.

media.title: D&D: Heroscape (Wizards Era)
media.description: Wizards of the Coast era Heroscape with D&D branding. Terrain-based miniatures game.
media.notes: Discontinued 2010. Original Heroscape revived by Hasbro 2022.

media.title: Advanced Dungeons & Dragons (DC Comics)
media.description: DC Comics AD&D series. Original characters. Forgotten Realms adjacent.
media.notes: 1988-1991. Out of print. Collected editions rare.

media.title: Dungeons & Dragons (IDW Publishing)
media.description: IDW ongoing series. Multiple arcs: Shadowplague, First Encounters, etc. 2010-2015.
media.notes: Collected in trade paperbacks. Jim Zub wrote later issues.

media.title: D&D: Legends of Baldur's Gate
media.description: IDW. Jim Zub. Minsc and Boo return. Baldur's Gate tie-in.
media.notes: Collected. Canon adjacent to video games.

media.title: D&D: Dungeons & Dragons (IDW 2020)
media.description: IDW relaunch. Fall of Tiamat arc. Jim Zub, Max Dunbar.
media.notes: Infernal Tides, Mindbreaker. Ongoing status uncertain.

media.title: Critical Role (Campaign 1)
media.description: Actual-play web series. Vox Machina. D&D 5E. Most influential actual-play ever. 2015-2017.
media.notes: Twitch/YouTube. Not official D&D canon but culturally central.

media.title: Critical Role (Campaign 2)
media.description: The Mighty Nein. Wildemount setting. 2018-2021.
media.notes: Explorer's Guide to Wildemount (2020) official D&D book.

media.title: Critical Role (Campaign 3)
media.description: Bells Hells. Marquet setting. 2021-2024.
media.notes: Concluded 2024. Campaign 4 announced.

media.title: Dimension 20: Fantasy High
media.description: Dropout actual-play. Brennan Lee Mulligan DM. High school fantasy setting. D&D 5E.
media.notes: Multiple seasons: Sophomore Year, Junior Year. Critical acclaim.

media.title: Acquisitions Incorporated (PAX)
media.description: Penny Arcade live-play. Jerry Holkins, Mike Krahulik, Scott Kurtz, later Chris Perkins DM.
media.notes: 2008-present. Official D&D book Acquisitions Incorporated (2019).

media.title: The Adventure Zone: Balance
media.description: McElroy family podcast. 5E. Most downloaded actual-play podcast. 2014-2017.
media.notes: Graphic novel adaptation from First Second Books.

media.title: D&D Beyond
media.description: Official digital toolset. Character builder, compendium, encounter builder. Acquired by Wizards 2018.
media.notes: Web, iOS, Android. Subscription model. Replaced previous digital tools.

media.title: D&D Insider
media.description: 4E digital suite. Character builder, compendium, Dungeon/Dragon magazines. Discontinued 2014.
media.notes: Defunct. Replaced by D&D Beyond. Archive available unofficially.

media.title: D&D: Dragon+ Magazine
media.description: Official digital magazine. Free PDFs. News, adventures, interviews. 2015-2020.
media.notes: Discontinued. Archives available.

media.title: Forgotten Realms Wiki
media.description: Community wiki. Comprehensive Forgotten Realms lore reference. 70,000+ articles.
media.notes: Not official but definitive fan resource.
